description_long: This object is a classic multi-barrel machine gun reminiscent of the Gatling gun design, featuring a cylindrical main body with a set of parallel rotating barrels. The body is primarily constructed from dark metal, accented with brass or bronze-colored end caps and rings. A prominent hand crank is attached to one side, used to operate the firing mechanism, while a large lever and a smaller handle are also visible for manual operation. Several loose brass cartridges are scattered nearby, indicating its use with metallic ammunition. The gun is supported by a robust frame with mounting brackets, suggesting it is designed to be fixed to a stand or vehicle. The overall construction is heavy-duty, with visible rivets and mechanical joints, emphasizing durability and industrial craftsmanship.
description: A heavy, multi-barrel machine gun with a hand crank, brass accents, and visible ammunition, built for robust operation and mounting.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cylindrical body, hand crank, lever, and brass end cap, with loose cartridges nearby.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the multiple barrels, circular barrel arrangement, and the mounting frame beneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the lever mechanism, mounting brackets, and the length of the barrels.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the large brass end cap, hand crank, and the rear mounting structure.
attribute_barrel_type: multi-barrel cylindrical cluster with smooth finish
